headline: Portuguese Settlement Malacca Faces Demolition for Reclamation Project
context: The 500-year-old Portuguese Settlement in Malacca faces partial demolition under the Melaka Waterfront Economic Zone reclamation project, affecting 1,800 residents of Portuguese-Eurasian descent.

cards.big: The Portuguese Settlement has existed in Malacca since 1511.
cards.sub: After 500 years, the reclamation project threatens to demolish portions of the settlement for commercial waterfront.

cards.big: 1,800 residents belong to one of Malaysia's smallest ethnic minorities.
cards.sub: The Portuguese-Eurasian community has no political representation at state or federal level.

cards.big: The settlement is a gazetted heritage zone under the Malacca Enactment 1988.
cards.sub: Reclamation approval appears to override heritage protection — no legal challenge has determined precedence.

cards.big: The settlement generates RM28M annually in heritage tourism.
cards.sub: Replacement with commercial waterfront may generate more revenue but destroys irreplaceable cultural capital.

cards.big: A 500-year-old community threatened by a developer. Heritage protection that cannot protect heritage is decorative.
Heritage zones must have legal supremacy over development approvals. If they do not, the designation is meaningless and communities built over centuries can be erased for a project cycle.
